explanation: Scenario number. S=1 means this is one simulated path.
Drawn tail-exponent value that controls the degree distribution.
Direct liquidity multiplier, equal to expected degree E[k].
Indirect multiplier, equal to E[k^2] / E[k] - 1.
Direct network liquidity without EWI-triggered policy support.
Indirect network liquidity without EWI-triggered policy support.
Lower-tail threshold used to identify liquidity-risk days.
True when baseline direct liquidity falls below the threshold.
True when the estimated early-warning indicator emits a signal.
True when policy support is active after an EWI signal.
Additional routing-capacity support added while support is active.
Shortfall of baseline direct liquidity below the risk threshold.
Shortfall of policy direct liquidity below the risk threshold.
